title: agropyron intermedium subsp. campestre
intro: Agropyron intermedium subsp. campestre, also known as crested wheatgrass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for wound healing and reducing inflammation. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, crested wheatgrass is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in capsule or powder form as a dietary supplement to support overall health and well-being.
